description_long: This is a large, double-headed battle axe with a menacing and rugged appearance. The axe features two wide, curved blades with jagged edges, both stained with what appears to be red paint or simulated blood, giving it a brutal, worn look. The central shaft is metallic and cylindrical, showing signs of wear and grime, and is capped at both ends with red, spiked metal fixtures. The handle is straight and reinforced, with a thick, flared pommel at the base for grip and balance. The head of the axe is adorned with a spiked, star-shaped finial, adding to its aggressive design. Some markings or symbols are visible on one blade, suggesting possible decorative or thematic elements.
description: A large, double-bladed battle axe with red-stained, jagged blades and a reinforced metal handle, featuring spiked details and visible wear.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, curved blades with red stains and the spiked finial at the top.
description_view_1: Side profile revealing the thinness of the blades and the cylindrical handle, with the spiked pommel and finial visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite faces of the blades, more red stains, and visible markings or symbols on the right blade.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the thinness of the axe and the alignment of the handle and blade assembly.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical metal handle with a thick, flared pommel and visible wear; likely steel with a rough grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, wide, curved blades with jagged edges, red stains, and a spiked star-shaped finial at the top; no beard or poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and reinforced with metal fixtures at the head and pommel.
attribute_visible_markings_or_labels: symbols or text on right blade
